{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CJEC3dPyoq9ixKJ7VSgAJL","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: import asyncio\\n2: from pathlib import Path\\n3: import types\\n4: import typing\\n5: from argparse import (\\n6:     ArgumentParser,\\n7:     ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\\n8:     BooleanOptionalAction,\\n9:     Namespace,\\n10: )\\n11: \\n12: \\n13: class FnToCLI:\\n14:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n15:     A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\\n16:     line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\\n17: \\n18:     This is _very_ basic; supports:\\n19:     * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\\n20:     * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\\n21:         * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\\n22:     * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\\n23:     * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\\n24:         * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\\n25:     * Type deduction of default values\\n26:     * Supports async functions automatically\\n27:     * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\\n28: \\n29:     Anything else will likely error :)\\n30: \\n31:     Example:\\n32:     if __name__ == '__main__':\\n33:         FnToCLI(my_func).run()\\n34:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n35: \\n36:     def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\\n37:         self.fn = fn\\n38:         arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\\n39:         annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\\n40:         defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n41:         num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\\n42:         default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\\n43:         defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\\n44:             arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\\n45:         }\\n46: \\n47:         docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\\n48:         arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\\n49:         self.parser = ArgumentParser(\\n50:             description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\\n51:             formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\\n52:         )\\n53:         self.args: Namespace | None = None\\n54:         for arg in arg_names:\\n55:             optional = arg in defaults\\n56:             cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\\n57: \\n58:             if arg in annotations:\\n59:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\\n60:             elif arg in defaults:\\n61:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\\n62:             else:\\n63:                 raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\\n64: \\n65:             # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\\n66:             arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\\n67: \\n68:             if optional:\\n69:                 opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\\n70:                 self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\\n71:             else:\\n72:                 self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\\n73: \\n74:     def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\\n75:         self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\\n76:         return self.args\\n77: \\n78:     def args_dict(self):\\n79:         if not self.args:\\n80:             self.parse_args()\\n81: \\n82:         return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\\n83: \\n84:     def run(self):\\n85:         args_dicts = self.args_dict()\\n86:         if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\\n87:             return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\\n88:         else:\\n89:             return self.fn(**args_dicts)\\n90: \\n91:     @staticmethod\\n92:     def parse_docs(docs):\\n93:         params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\\n94:         params = [p.strip() for p in params]\\n95:         params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\\n96:         return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\\n97: \\n98:     @staticmethod\\n99:     def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\\n100:         if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\\n101:             return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\\n102:                 next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\\n103:             )\\n104:         if typ == bool:\\n105:             return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\\n106: \\n107:         simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\\n108:         if typ in simple_types:\\n109:             return {'type': typ}\\n110: \\n111:         if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\\n112:             subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\\n113:             if subtype in simple_types:\\n114:                 return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\\n115: \\n116:         if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\\n117:             return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\\n118:         raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\\n119: \\n120:     @staticmethod\\n121:     def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:\\n122:         return (\\n123:             (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))\\n124:             and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)\\n125:             and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2\\n126:         )\\n127: \\n128: \\n129: if __name__ == '__main__':\\n130: \\n131:     def fn(nums: list[int]):\\n132:         print(sum(nums))\\n133: \\n134:     cli = FnToCLI(fn)\\n135:     cli.run()\\n136: asyncio\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport types\nimport typing\nfrom argparse import (\n    ArgumentParser,\n    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n    BooleanOptionalAction,\n    Namespace,\n)\n\n\nclass FnToCLI:\n    \"\"\"\n    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n\n    This is _very_ basic; supports:\n    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\n    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\n    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\n        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n    * Type deduction of default values\n    * Supports async functions automatically\n    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n        self.fn = fn\n        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = None\n        for arg in arg_names:\n            optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\n\n            if optional:\n                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n asyncio\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport types\nimport typing\nfrom argparse import (\n    ArgumentParser,\n    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n    BooleanOptionalAction,\n    Namespace,\n)\n\n\nclass FnToCLI:\n    \"\"\"\n    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n\n    This is _very_ basic; supports:\n    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\n    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\n    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\n        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n    * Type deduction of default values\n    * Supports async functions automatically\n    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n        self.fn = fn\n        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = None\n        for arg in arg_names:\n            optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\n\n            if optional:\n                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n        return self.args\n\n    def args_dict(self):\n        if not self.args:\n            self.parse_args()\n\n        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\n\n    def run(self):\n        args_dicts = self.args_dict()\n        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\n            return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\n        else:\n            return self.fn(**args_dicts)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def parse_docs(docs):\n        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\n        params = [p.strip() for p in params]\n        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\n        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n            )\n        if typ == bool:\n            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n\n        simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n        if typ in simple_types:\n            return {'type': typ}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n            subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n                return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:\n        return (\n            (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))\n            and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)\n            and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2\n        )\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n\n    def fn(nums: list[int]):\n        print(sum(nums))\n\n    cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n    cli.run()\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01588q4U5rnZESevHroMdLXZ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GRzgKmxK3M15y5qweh1zhA","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/tests/test_fn_to_cli.py","content":"from argparse import BooleanOptionalAction\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport typing\nfrom scripts.solr_builder.solr_builder.fn_to_cli import FnToCLI\n\n\nclass TestFnToCLI:\n    def test_full_flow(self):\n        def fn(works: list[str], solr_url: str | None = None):\n            \"\"\"\n            Do some magic!\n            :param works: These are works\n            :param solr_url: This is solr url\n            \"\"\"\n\n        cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n        assert cli.parser.description.strip() == 'Do some magic!'\n        assert '--solr-url' in cli.parser.format_usage()\n\n    def test_parse_docs(self):\n        docs = \"\"\"\n        :param a: A\n        :param b: B\n        :param c: C\n        \"\"\"\n        assert FnToCLI.parse_docs(docs) == {'a': 'A', 'b': 'B', 'c': 'C'}\n\n        docs = \"\"\"\n        Some function description\n\n        :param a: A asdfas\n        \"\"\"\n        assert FnToCLI.parse_docs(docs) == {'a': 'A asdfas'}\n\n    def test_type_to_argparse(self):\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(int) == {'type': int}\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(typing.Optional[int]) == {  # noqa: UP007\n            'type': int\n        }\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(bool) == {\n            'type': bool,\n            'action': BooleanOptionalAction,\n        }\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(typing.Literal['a', 'b']) == {\n            'choices': ('a', 'b'),\n        }\n\n    def test_is_optional(self):\n        assert FnToCLI.is_optional(typing.Optional[int])  # noqa: UP007\n        assert not FnToCLI.is_optional(int)\n\n    def test_lists(self):\n        def fn(nums: list[int]):\n            return sum(nums)\n\n        cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n        cli.parse_args(['1', '2', '3'])\n        assert cli.run() == 6\n\n    def test_paths(self):\n        def fn(files: list[Path] | None = None):\n            if not files:\n                return None\n            return [isinstance(f, Path) for f in files]\n\n        cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n        cli.parse_args(['--files', 'path1', 'path2'])\n        assert cli.run() == [True, True]\n\n        cli.parse_args([])\n        assert cli.run() is None\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1569,"cache_read_input_tokens":47679,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1569},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"082915c8-20c5-4383-9694-57d5b3cbc636","uuid":"18e17320-d0de-4d81-906a-b24edffbcd56"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GRzgKmxK3M15y5qweh1zhA","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /workspace/scripts/solr_builder/tests/test_fn_to_cli.py"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"082915c8-20c5-4383-9694-57d5b3cbc636","uuid":"0cb3e8e3-c924-4926-b34c-2b82110bd8fc","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/tests/test_fn_to_cli.py","content":"from argparse import BooleanOptionalAction\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport typing\nfrom scripts.solr_builder.solr_builder.fn_to_cli import FnToCLI\n\n\nclass TestFnToCLI:\n    def test_full_flow(self):\n        def fn(works: list[str], solr_url: str | None = None):\n            \"\"\"\n            Do some magic!\n            :param works: These are works\n            :param solr_url: This is solr url\n            \"\"\"\n\n        cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n        assert cli.parser.description.strip() == 'Do some magic!'\n        assert '--solr-url' in cli.parser.format_usage()\n\n    def test_parse_docs(self):\n        docs = \"\"\"\n        :param a: A\n        :param b: B\n        :param c: C\n        \"\"\"\n        assert FnToCLI.parse_docs(docs) == {'a': 'A', 'b': 'B', 'c': 'C'}\n\n        docs = \"\"\"\n        Some function description\n\n        :param a: A asdfas\n        \"\"\"\n        assert FnToCLI.parse_docs(docs) == {'a': 'A asdfas'}\n\n    def test_type_to_argparse(self):\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(int) == {'type': int}\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(typing.Optional[int]) == {  # noqa: UP007\n            'type': int\n        }\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(bool) == {\n            'type': bool,\n            'action': BooleanOptionalAction,\n        }\n        assert FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(typing.Literal['a', 'b']) == {\n            'choices': ('a', 'b'),\n        }\n\n    def test_is_optional(self):\n        assert FnToCLI.is_optional(typing.Optional[int])  # noqa: UP007\n        assert not FnToCLI.is_optional(int)\n\n    def test_lists(self):\n        def fn(nums: list[int]):\n            return sum(nums)\n\n        cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n        cli.parse_args(['1', '2', '3'])\n        assert cli.run() == 6\n\n    def test_paths(self):\n        def fn(files: list[Path] | None = None):\n            if not files:\n                return None\n            return [isinstance(f, Path) for f in files]\n\n        cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n        cli.parse_args(['--files', 'path1', 'path2'])\n        assert cli.run() == [True, True]\n\n        cli.parse_args([])\n        assert cli.run() is None\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

choices)\n        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n    * Type deduction of default values\n    * Supports async functions automatically\n    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n       defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n        return self.args\n\n    def args_dict(self):\n        if not self.args:\n            self.parse_args()\n\n        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\n\n    def run(self):\n        args_dicts = self.args_dict()\n        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\n            return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\n        else:\n            return self.fn(**args_dicts)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def parse_docs(docs):\n        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\n        params = [p.strip() for p in params]\n        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\n        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def simple_types:\n            return {'type': typ}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n            subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"082915c8-20c5-4383-9694-57d5b3cbc636","uuid":"048a7f60-79ff-442c-8f1b-921905ab3a8b","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/scripts/solr_builder/solr_builder/fn_to_cli.py","content":"import asyncio\nimport types\nimport typing\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom argparse import (\n    ArgumentParser,\n    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n    BooleanOptionalAction,\n    Namespace,\n)\n\n\nclass FnToCLI:\n    \"\"\"\n    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n\n    This is _very_ basic; supports:\n    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\n    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\n    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\n        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n    * Type deduction of default values\n    * Supports async functions automatically\n    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n        self.fn = fn\n        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = None\n        for arg in arg_names:\n            optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\n\n            if optional:\n                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n        return self.args  # type: ignore[return-value]\n\n    def args_dict(self):\n        if not self.args:\n            self.parse_args()\n\n        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\n\n    def run(self):\n        args_dicts = self.args_dict()\n        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\n            return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\n        else:\n            return self.fn(**args_dicts)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def parse_docs(docs):\n        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\n        params = [p.strip() for p in params]\n        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\n        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n            )\n        if typ == bool:\n            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n\n        simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n        if typ in simple_types:\n            return {'type': typ}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n            subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n                return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:\n        return (\n            (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))\n            and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)\n            and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2\n        )\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n\n    def fn(nums: list[int]):\n        print(sum(nums))\n\n    cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n    cli.run()\n","numLines":136,"startLine":1,"totalLines":136}}}
